msDesc.head: Book of Hours in Latin and English (Use of Sarum); England, s. xv (second half) and s. xvi

msItem.title: Book of Hours, Use of Sarum, in Latin and English

msItem.note: Easter table in English for 1545-1580.

Calendar: standard Sarum calendar, with all Thomas of Canterbury’s feasts erased (December feast, January Octave, July translation, and Nativity), as are all ‘papa’ designations; does include Cuthburga (Cuthburge virginis) in August (founder and abbess of double monastery at Wimborne, Dorset), who is not in standard Sarum Calendar as printed in The Sarum Missal, ed. J. Wickham Legg, Oxford 1916
[Fifteen Prayers on the Passion]: Quindecim orationes ad passionem Christi. Domine Ihesu Christi, eterna dulcedo te ... Each prayer finishes with rubric, ‘Pater Noster. Ave Maria’.

[Meditations on the Cross] with the following incipits: Adoro te domine Ihesu Christe in cruce pendentem et coronam spineam in capite portantem; Adoro te domine Ihesu Christe in cruce vulneratum felle et aceto potatum; Adoro te domine Ihesu Christe in sepulcro positum; Domine Ihesu Christe pastor bone iustos, conserva; Domine ihesu Christe Rogo te propter illam maximam amaritudinem passionis (with R', Adoramus te Christe et benedicimus tibi; Benignissime domine Ihesu Christe respice super me.
[Prayer of St. Bernardino]: Oracio sancti Bernardini. O bone Ihesu, o dulcis Ihesu fili Marie virginis plena misericordia et veritatem
Invocation to St. Appollonia, against toothache: Virgo Christ egregia ... ne morbo vexamur dencium .
Prayer: Omnipotens sempiterne deus spes et corona omnibus
Prayer: These Prayers folowyne ought to be saide er ye departe oute of youre chambre at youre first uprysynge. Auxiliatrix ///lis michi trinitas sancta
Prayer: Wan thou goest first oute of thy hous blesse the sayenge thus, Crux triumphalis domini nostri Ihesu Christi
Prayer: Oracio. Deus qui tres magos orientales ...
Prayer: Whan thou entrest to the chirche, saye thus: Domine in multitudine misericordie tue
Prayer: Whan thou takest holy water saye thus, Aqua benedicta sit michi salus [read: salvus] et vita
Prayer: Whanne thou begynnest to praye, thus begynne knelynge, Discendit a me maligni et scrutabor mandatum dei
Prayers: a) Oracio. O Bone Ihesue, tu novisti and b) Oracio. Ascendat ad te domine deus oracio mea
Pater Noster, Ave Maria and Creed.
Prayer: Confiteor in tibi domine Ihesu Christe omnia peccata mea, followed by a standard Sarum Litany
Prayer: Suscipera domine domine deus omnipotens

Hours of Virgin (Sarum Use): Hore beate Marie virginis. Domine labia ... [fol. 39r, Lauds followed by Suffrages to Holy Spirit, Trinity, Cross, Michael, John the Baptist, Peter and Paul, Andrew, John the Evangelist, Luke, Stephen; on fols 42r-v, James of Compostella added in another hand]; each Hour ends with meditations on the Passion, De sancta cruce and De dolore virginis.

Prayers on the Compassion of Mary: a) Oracio. Domine sancte Ihesu fili dulcis virginis Marie qui pro nobis mortem in cruce tollerasti b) De domina nostra. Salve regina misericordie c) Oracio. Omnipotens sempiterne deus qui gloriose virginis d) De sancta Maria. Gaude virgo mater e) Oracio. Deus qui beatissima virgine Maria in conceptu et partu virginitate
Seven Joys of Mary: Septem gaudiis beate marie virginis. Gaude flore virginale honoresque ...
O intemerata, entitled De sancta Maria oracio (masculine form).
Obsecro te, entitled Ad Virginem Mariam (masculine form).

Prayers with the following incipits: a) A. Stella celi extirpavit b) Oratio. Deus misericordie, deus pietatis c) Alia [oratio]. Ave sanctissimum et preciosissimum corpus d) Alia oratio. In presencia sacro sancti corporis e) Oratio. Domine Ihesu Christe qui hanc sacratissimem carem f) De sancta trinitate. Sancta trinitas vivus deus miserere nobis g) Deus qui superbis resistis h) Deus qui liberasti Susannam de falso crimine i) Domine Ihesu Christi qui me creasti j) Domine Ihesu Christi qui solus es sapiencia
Prayers : In honore trium regnum. a) T[read R]ex Iapsar, rex Melchior b) Deus illuminator omnium gentium c) Oracio. Deus qui tres magos orientales.
four prayers in English: a) O glorious Jesu, o mekest iesu; b) O the most swetest spouse; c) Oracio ad sanctissima Trinitatem. O blessed trinyte; d) O lorde god almyghty, all seynge
Mediation on the Body of Christ: Oracio ante ymaginem corporis Christi. Conditor celi et terre
Seven Penitential Psalms
Fifteen Gradual Psalms (nos. 119-33)
Prayer: Memento domine David et omnis mansuetudinis eius
a) Ecce quam bonum et quam iocundum habitate fratres; b) Ecce nunc benedicite

Prayers, each rubricated Oracio and with the following incipits : a) Deus cui proprium est miserare (sic) semper et parcere, suscipe deprecationem nostram; b) Omnipotens sempiterne deus qui facis mirabilia magna: c) Deus qui caritatis dona per gratiam; d) Deus a quo sancta desideria recta consilia; e) Ineffabilem ?misteriam tuam ?quesumus domine clementer; f) Fidelium deus omnium conditor et redemptor; g) Pietate tua queris domine; h) Per horum omnium sanctorum angelorum

Verses of St. Bernard : Versus sancti Bernardi. Illumina oculos meos ne unquam obdormiam in morte ; Peto domine Ihesu largire michi in amore tuo (see Leroquais, LH, I, p. xxx and Searle, Fitzwilliam, p. 1)
Office of the Dead, Use of Sarum: Vigilie mortuorum. Placebo.

Prayers, rubricated Oracio and with the following incipits: a) Deus cui proprium est misereri semper et parcare; b) Omnipotens sempiterne deus; c) Deus cuius misericordie non est munerus; d) Inclina domine aurem tuam ad preces nostras; e) Arimibus queris domine omnium fidelium defunctorum

?Psalm extracts for use between All Souls and Easter: In feriis inter festum animarum et pascha Ps. Extracts beginning: a) Voce mea ad dominum clamavi; b) Retribue servo tuo; c) Legem pone michi domine viam; d) Memor este verbi tui servo tuo; e) Porcio mea domine dixi custodire legem tuam; f) Bonitatem fecisti cum servo tuo; g) Manus tue domine fecerunt me; h) Defecit in salutare tuam anima mea; i) In eternum domine verbum tuum; j) Quomodo dilexi legem tuam; k) Iniquos odio habui; l) Feci iudicium et iustitciam; m) Miribilia testimonia; n) Iustus es domine et rectum; o) Clamavi in toto corde; p) Vide humilitatem meam; q) Principes presenti sunt; r) Appropinquet deprecatio mea in conspectu tuo; s) Domine probasti me; followed by:
Oracio. Tibi domine commendamus animam famuli tui;
Alia oracio. Avete omnes anime fideles quorum

Meditation in English on an image no longer present in MS; To them that before this ymage of pyte devoutely say. V. pater noster .V. Ave maria and a Credo pytously beholdynge these armes of christes pass[ioun]

acephalous text, starting ‘matris mee vite proiectus sum ex utero. De ventris matris mee deus meus es tu ne discesseris a me ... with following Psalm incipits starting on fol. 165v (eached marked Ps): a) Dominus regit me et nichil michi deerit in loco pascue; b) Domini est terra et plenitudo eius; c) Ad te domine levavi animam meam; d) Iudica me domine; e) Dominus illuminacio mea

Prayers: a) Oracio. Interveniat pro nobis domine Ihesu christe apud tuam clemenciam; b) Oracio. Beati Iohannis apostoli tui et ewangeliste [sic]; c) De sancta Maria. Sancta Maria regina celi et terre mater domini nostri Ihesu Christi in sanctas ???? venerabilis manus; d) Oracio. Ave benigne Ihesu gratia plenus misericordia tecum
Psalter of St. Jerome ; Psalterium beati Iheronimi. Verba mea auribus percipe ...
Prayer: Oracio. Omnipotens sempiterne deus clemenciam tuam suppliciter deprecor fol. 190v, blank

Prayers in English and Latin: a) A devoute prayer. O my soverayne lorde Jesu the veray sonne of allmyghty god and of the moost clene and glorious virgyn Mary; b) Deus propicius est iiiio peccatori vel peccatrici. I thanke the also with all my herte ... my moost gracious lorde for the benefites c) Oracio. Deus in cuius manu sunt corda regum; d) Oracio. Deus qui nos patrem et matrem honorare precepisti
Prayer of Bede on the Seven Words: Domine qui septem verba in ultimo vite tue

Prayer to the Trinity: Oracio devotissima ad sanctam trinitatem. Adoro te sancta et individua trinitas ... O beata trinitas benedicat te deus simplex et trine omnis spiritus angelicus et humanus; dicens sine intercessione [read: cessatione????] Sanctus, Sanctus, Sanctus, Dominus deus Sabaoth, Pleni sunt celi et terre gloria tua. Osanna in excelsis. Amen.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliated inaccurately in modern pencil; folio numbers in this description are one less than that written in the MS.
supportDesc.collation: iiii [flyleaves] + i [Easter table] + I(6) [Calendar; stub of singleton for Easter table appears at the end of this quire]; thereafter too tightly bound to collate, with no quire markings or catchwords. One folio, once present between what are now fols 163v and 164r, containing an image on the recto and text on the verso, has been excised. Fol. 1r, the first page of the Calendar, is very worn and was clearly the front cover for some time.

layoutDesc.layout:
Written space
120 75 ; ruled in faded red crayon, 19 lines per page, but fols 92r-100r and 191r-201v (Hand 2 stints) have written space 120 x 70mm, thicker lines of ruling in pink ink, 18 lines per page.

handDesc.handNote: Hand 1, main text hand, fols 7r-91v and 101r-190r: gothic bookhand with a slightly barbed aspect; second half, s. xv.
Hand 2, main texts on fols 92r-100r and 191r-201v, gothic bookhand but in darker ink and with squarer aspect; possibly slightly later than Hand 1
Hand 3, Calendar, fol. 1r-6v, a good quality rounded Gothic bookhand with some barbed seriphs.
Hand 4, writes in margins of fols 84v-85r, s. xv bastard secretary of good quality
Hand 5, s. xvi hand writes Easter table.

decoDesc.decoNote:
Full page miniatures with full acanthus and berry borders in blue, red, green and brown.
fol. 7r, Christ in Judgement
fol. 24r (Matins), Annunciation
fol. 31v (Lauds), Visitation
fol. 47r (Prime) Nativity (incomplete)
fols. 52v (Terce), Annunciation to the Shepherds
fol. 56v (Sext), Adoration of the Magi
fol. 60v (None), Presentation in the Temple
fol. 64v (Vespers), Massacre of the Innocents
fol. 70v (Compline), Flight into Egypt
fol. 101r (Penitential Psalms), Last Judgement
fol. 116v (Office of the Dead), Christ raises Lazarus from the dead
fol. 177r, (Psalter of St. Jerome), Jerome kneels before Cross

Other miniatures with borders, approx. 8 lines high:
fol. 15r (Meditations on the Cross), Three Crosses
fol. 16v (Prayer of St. Bernard), Christ Child
fol. 19r (Prayers), God the father supports crucified Christ.
There follow seven images to accompany the Suffrage to the Holy Cross at the end of each Hour (apart from Matins), taken from the Passion cycle:
fol. 45r (Lauds), Arrest of Christ
fol. 51r (Prime), Christ before Pilate
fol. 55r (Terce), Crown of Thorns
fol. 59v (Sext), Christ carries the Cross
fol. 63r (None), Crucifixion
fol. 69r (Vespers), Deposition
fol. 74r (Compline), Entombment.
fol. 75v (Salve Regina), Christ Child in glory with Madonna
fol. 79v (O intemerata), Visitation? or Mary kneeling in prayer with devout layperson
fol. 81v (Obsecro te), Pietà
fol. 88r (Prayer on the Trinity), Trinity
fol. 115r (Verses of St. Bernard), White friar (Bernard) kneeling before Madonna and child.
Major initials of 6 lines, fols. 7r, 24r, 31v, 47, 52v, 56v, 60v, 64v, 70v, 101r, 116v, 177r. Minor initials of 2 lines.
Plain blue initials with red penwork.

history.provenance: Jacobi Sutton armiger, top edge, front inner, rear pastedown, s.xv/xvi hand, liber Jacobi Sutton armigeri. JSutton (JS mingled to form a monogram) in black and red ink, same inscription in faded black ink inside front board.

Throckmorton family: pasted inside front board is the quartered arms with eagle and Sr. Robt. Throckmorton Bart. with ?Weston shelfmark, J3 No. 10. Recto of flyleaf v has ‘Ex libri[s?] Dni. Rob. Throckmorton Baronetti. Biblioth: Weston:’.
Offered for sale, Christie's, 20 Dec. 1972, as part of lots 204, 206-211, but withdrawn before the sale.
Sold to the National Trust in 1991.
history.acquisition: Deposited by the National Trust in 2026.

recordHist.source: Abbreviated from a draft description by Suzanne Reynolds.
